cover.title: THE MIST
cover.subtitle: The one who believes in people to the end, even when the evidence has already arrived.
cover.tagline: You understand too much — and sometimes protect yourself too late.

profile_scores.caption: High warmth and tolerance, low drive and expression — you understand to the end, believe to the end, and sometimes protect yourself too late.

short_summary: You don't give up on people easily — once you trust someone, you keep believing in the good side you once saw.
Your pain often comes from knowing something is wrong, but waiting too long anyway.
What changes love for you is learning to include yourself in the relationship, not only the other person's story.

full_report.headline: THE MIST
full_report.intro: You don't give up on people easily. Once you like someone, you keep watching them for a long time, and once you trust someone, you trust them for a long time. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why you understand people so deeply, why you leave too late, and how love changes when you finally take your own side too.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You don't give up on people easily.
Once you like someone, you keep watching them for a long time. Once you trust someone, you trust them for a long time.
So people think you're docile. But that's actually not true.
Your will is stronger than people think.
Once you make up your mind, it lasts. You don't turn your back easily either.
The problem is that strength sometimes gets used in the wrong place.
You hold onto someone you should let go of.
You try to understand a relationship that's already over.
You try to flip an answer that's already been decided.
Because when you look at someone, you don't just see one action.
You see their circumstances too. Their emotions too. Their wounds too.
So you're slow to leave. Because you understand too much.
chapters.profile_note: High Warmth · High Tolerance, Low Drive · Low Expression — You understand to the end, believe to the end, and sometimes protect yourself too late.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Repeat
body: At first there's a small discomfort. Something's slightly off. Something snags. Something feels a little hurt.
But you let it go. Because it could happen.
It happens again. You understand it again. You find a reason for it again.
Then at some point, the evidence starts piling up.
But you still believe. Because people can make mistakes. Because there could be a reason. Because there were good moments too.
And in the end, you're the last to realize: you were the one holding onto the relationship more than they were.
chapters.highlight: The reason you get hurt isn't that you don't know. It's that you knew, and waited far too long anyway.
chapters.pattern_name: THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT CURVE

chart.title: THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT CURVE
chart.description: The pattern of keeping giving second chances even after doubt has set in.

chapters.title: What You Got Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: No. It's not that you trust people. You trust, all the way to the end, the good side of them you once saw.
items.truth_short: You keep trusting the good side you once saw.

items.belief: Let's just wait a little longer.
items.truth: Hmm. In most cases, you've already waited plenty. This isn't waiting — you're putting off a decision.

items.belief: I'm not someone with much desire.
items.truth: No, you are. You want to be loved. You want to be treated as precious. You want to be a priority. You just endure it for far too long.
items.truth_short: You do want to be loved — you just endure too long.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're weak to people with a complicated backstory.
A hard past. A complicated situation. Unresolved feelings.
Hearing things like that makes sense to you. Why they act that way. Why they say what they say.
But here's the problem. Understanding and allowing are different things.
chapters.highlight: Someone who makes you understand gives you reasons. Someone who loves you gives you consideration.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't an even bigger heart for understanding. You already have more than enough.
What you need is one single question.
I get that this person is having a hard time. But do I have to keep hurting because of it?
Once you start asking this question, a lot changes.
It's not that you love them less. You're finally including yourself, too, in that relationship.
chapters.action_sentence: This time, change begins when you include yourself in the relationship too.

chapters.title: Where to Go From Here
body: You know how to forgive people.
You know how to wait. You know how to trust again.
But what you'll learn next is how to draw the line.
Not treating understanding someone and getting hurt because of them as the same thing.
Once you have that, relationships become far healthier.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time learning how to understand people. Now you're learning how to protect yourself faster.

chapters.title: The Type You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone carrying a lot of wounds
items.description: They tell you about their childhood on the second date. They apologize for being difficult. You see their pain before you see their behavior. But being hurt in the past and treating you well in the present are two different things.

items.type_title: Someone who hasn't sorted themselves out
items.description: They still talk about their ex. Their life is always about to become stable next month. You keep waiting for the version of them that never fully arrives. Waiting beside a problem isn't the same as solving it.

items.type_title: Someone who's good to you once in a while
items.description: They disappear for days, then suddenly show up with kindness that melts your heart. Every time, you think, "Maybe this time will be different." But occasional warmth and everyday consideration are not the same love.
At first they say, "You always get me." Later, they stop apologizing because they assume you'll understand again. Appreciation quietly turns into expectation.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs were there pretty early. You just kept explaining them away more than anyone.

signal_strength.label: You understand on their behalf, instead of reacting to uncomfortable behavior
You think of their circumstances before your own hurt feelings
You keep forgiving the same repeating problem
You see their potential more than their actual behavior

chapters.core_signal: The evidence keeps piling up, but so does your hope, right alongside it.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like to You

checklist.title: They're considerate without needing it explained.
checklist.description: You don't have to make them understand every time.

checklist.title: Apologies turn into action.
checklist.description: It's not just the same words repeated.

checklist.title: Your feelings matter too.
checklist.description: It's not always only about their story.

checklist.title: You feel reassured.
checklist.description: You don't have to keep waiting.

chapters.title: What Happens in Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: You're weak to people who need explaining. You feel like you understand why they're like that, why they're struggling, why they're not ready yet. So you keep waiting. But a relationship isn't a test of how well you can understand someone.
sections.who_steadies_you: On the other hand, there's someone who fits you well. Someone who doesn't complicate things. Someone who shows it when they like you, fixes it when they're sorry, and keeps their promises. This kind of person doesn't drain your energy. They reassure you.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: This time, you'll see it sooner. Things you might have only admitted months later before — this time you'll see within weeks. Hurt. Repetition. Disappointment. And a familiar voice will rise up inside you: still, let's wait just a little longer.
sections.next_signs: The sign will be the moment you notice yourself explaining their behavior before you admit your own hurt.
sections.try_this: This time, don't look at the other person. Look at yourself. Just once. Ask this question: If my friend went through exactly the same thing, what would I tell them? Then write down the answer. No excuses. No explanations. Exactly as it is.
sections.final_line: This time, take your own side first, not theirs. That answer is the answer.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to understand people. What's left is learning how to protect yourself before understanding turns into self-abandonment.
action_items: This week, ask whether understanding someone is also costing you something.
Write down what you would tell a friend in the same situation.
Watch whether apologies become action, not just another reason to wait.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to understand people. This report covered why you keep finding meaning even in relationships that have already shown you the answer — and how you can learn to protect yourself faster.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
